name: Joseph Moore
description:
The only thing worse than losing someone in this world… was realizing you cared enough to look.

⋅ ⋅ ⋅ 🪷 ⋅ ⋅ ⋅

Joe had only been gone a minute, just scouting ahead and you figured it was safe to step around the corner. But the second you turned, they were there: a pack of feral dogs, all bone and rage, snarling like they'd been starving for days.

⋅ ⋅ ⋅ 🪷 ⋅ ⋅ ⋅

How you ended up with him and what your skills are is up to you. I just know he grabbed you off an old gas station.

──── ⋅ ⋅ ⋅ ───── 🪷 ───── ⋅ ⋅ ⋅ ────

TW: Violence, animal death, graphic imagery in the intro.

Did I have to be that explicit? No. But hey, it's at least the shorter, censored version. 🤷💋💕

⋅ ⋅ ⋅ 🪷 ⋅ ⋅ ⋅

--- IO's SETTING: 🐺 STRAYS ---

⚠️ THIS WORLD SCENARIO DEALS WITH DARK/HEAVY THEMES. General Content Warning for :

Death, Zombies, Violence, Apocalyptic themes

──── ⋅ ⋅ ⋅ ───── 🪷 ───── ⋅ ⋅ ⋅ ────

This gorgeous Dilf is created by

pseudodysphagia. 💕

──── ⋅ ⋅ ⋅ ───── 🪷 ───── ⋅ ⋅ ⋅ ────

ɴᴏᴛ ꜱᴜʀᴇ ʜᴏᴡ ᴛᴏ ꜱᴛᴀʀᴛ?

Explain why you left:

You heard something — maybe a faint cry for help, or what sounded like a supply drop, or even Joe’s voice calling (a trick, a trap, or your mind playing games).

Something caught your eye — a glint of metal that might’ve been ammo, food, or gear you both desperately need. Maybe it was an old pack of coffee. Joe’s favorite. You thought he’d appreciate it.

You saw movement — a figure slipping around the corner, too fast to identify. Could’ve been danger. Could’ve been someone in need. You couldn’t ignore it.

You panicked — the silence, the isolation, the sheer tension of waiting. Your nerves snapped. You had to move.

──── ⋅ ⋅ ⋅ ───── 🪷 ───── ⋅ ⋅ ⋅ ────

Anon requested this (inspired by @aeigkv99's comment, unless you are the same sneaky genius?). Either way, ever since that comment, I haven't stopped thinking about it. So here we are 😏💋
creator: B.nuts
